transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder, V-Log, July 1st, 2019, Monday at 3.39 p.m.
Last night I got to sleep at maybe 11.10 or something.
I'm diodalized, and I got up at like 5, so I got like, you know, my normal hour sleep at night.
So, yeah, so yesterday I wanted to finish tracing this trail.
I made a trail around Atlantis that was 91 kilometers, 56 miles, because I was curving it.
I can't remember what the straight road around it was, but, and I didn't want to do it with a line tool.
So I did it, I did it with a line tool first, but then I wanted to do it with the polyglon tool.
So the way that you make a road with a polyglon tool is you, you trace around one side and then you trace the other side.
Um, and that's really a big polyglon if you're doing a big road.
So I did the polyglon all the way around, and then I turned around and I was going back, and I was like 7, 8ths of the way through, and then it just disappeared.
First the color went away, and then it disappeared.
So then I saved it as was and restarted Google Earth and then restarted my computer, and it was just gone.
So I didn't delete the file, but it's gone, it won't open it.
So I guess the computer bonked.
Um, I'll keep that file there in case if I get another better computer later, I'll see if that computer can open it.
I don't know, or maybe it's corrupted, I don't know.
So I can't use the polyglon tool to show my mountain bike trail.
All I can do is use the normal line, so that's fine.
It wasted a lot of time.
It was like 4 hours, 4 solid hours at least that I was working on that.
It was crazy.
I was sitting like, what the hell am I doing?
